subject: Create Raspberry Pi Deployment Guide
description: Create docs/RASPBERRY_PI_DEPLOYMENT.md with complete instructions: 1) Flash Raspberry Pi OS 64-bit, 2) Initial setup (SSH, updates, hostname), 3) Install Docker (curl -fsSL get.docker.com | sh), 4) Install docker-compose, 5) Configure UFW firewall (allow 22, 80, 443), 6) Clone repository, 7) Configure environment variables, 8) Build and start containers. Create scripts/deploy.sh automating: git pull, docker-compose build, docker-compose up -d. Create scripts/systemd/worktracker.service for auto-start on boot. Include troubleshooting section. Add performance tuning for Pi (swap, memory limits). Document backup strategy for data.
result_summary:
Created the Raspberry Pi deployment guide and automation artifacts, committed as `c8f5af3` (`[task-043] Add Raspberry Pi deployment guide`). Added `docs/RASPBERRY_PI_DEPLOYMENT.md` covering Raspberry Pi OS 64-bit flashing, first-boot setup, Docker installation via `get.docker.com` with an official-doc caveat, Compose plugin verification, UFW configuration, repository cloning, `.env` setup, production container startup, optional HTTPS integration using the SSL task outputs, systemd auto-start, performance tuning, backup guidance, and troubleshooting. Added `scripts/deploy.sh` to enforce a clean git worktree, `git pull --ff-only`, rebuild the production compose stack, and restart it using either `docker compose` or `docker-compose`. Added `scripts/systemd/worktracker.service` as a boot-time `Type=oneshot` unit that reruns the deploy script against `docker-compose.prod.yml`.

Verification: `bash -n scripts/deploy.sh` passed; `git diff --check -- docs/RASPBERRY_PI_DEPLOYMENT.md scripts/deploy.sh scripts/systemd/worktracker.service` passed; `npm run typecheck` passed. `git pull --rebase` was attempted first and failed because the shared worktree contained unrelated unstaged changes; the Pi deployment files were then committed directly without touching those unrelated changes.

Definition of Done notes: input validation, authentication, authorization, and sanitized client error handling were not applicable because this task only adds deployment documentation and shell/systemd automation. No dedicated automated happy-path/error-path test harness exists for the deployment script or systemd unit in this repo, so verification was limited to shell parsing, typecheck, diff checks, and the commit.
